title: Urban 3D Reconstruction
summary: Turning aerial imagery and LiDAR into accurate, textured 3D city models.
description: We develop end-to-end pipelines that fuse multi-view aerial imagery with airborne LiDAR to reconstruct buildings, vegetation, and terrain at city scale. Research questions include robust multi-view stereo, mesh regularisation under noisy observations, and keeping models consistent as new data arrives.

publications.title: Regularised Mesh Reconstruction from Fused Aerial LiDAR and Imagery
publications.venue: ISPRS Journal of Photogrammetry and Remote Sensing

title: Deep Learning for SAR
summary: Learning-based despeckling, InSAR analysis, and change detection for radar data.
description: Synthetic Aperture Radar sees through clouds and works day or night, but its speckle noise and complex geometry make interpretation hard. We design neural methods — often self-supervised — for despeckling, interferometric phase estimation, and detecting change between acquisitions, with applications in deformation monitoring.

publications.title: Self-Supervised Despeckling of Single-Look Complex SAR
publications.venue: IEEE Transactions on Geoscience and Remote Sensing

title: Semantic LiDAR & Digital Twins
summary: Giving point clouds meaning, then keeping a living digital twin in sync.
description: Beyond geometry, we label every point — facade, road, tree, vehicle — so a 3D scene becomes queryable. We then study how to maintain a digital twin over time as the real environment changes, fusing repeated mobile-mapping scans into a single, up-to-date semantic model.

title: Neural Implicit Surfaces
summary: Continuous surface reconstruction from oblique aerial photogrammetry.
description: We represent buildings and terrain as neural implicit fields rather than discrete meshes, learning a continuous signed-distance or occupancy function directly from multi-view oblique imagery. This yields watertight, level-of-detail-adaptive surfaces that interpolate cleanly across sparsely observed facades.

title: Cross-View Geo-Localisation
summary: Matching ground-level photos to satellite tiles to recover where they were taken.
description: Given a single street-level photograph, we estimate its geographic location by matching it against a database of overhead satellite tiles. The challenge is the extreme viewpoint gap between ground and aerial imagery; we learn a shared embedding in which co-located views are close despite that gap.

title: Multi-Temporal Change Detection
summary: Spotting what changed between SAR acquisitions with transformer attention.
description: By aligning long stacks of Synthetic Aperture Radar acquisitions and attending across time, we detect and characterise change — new construction, subsidence, flooding — while separating real surface change from speckle and seasonal nuisance variation.
